title: Heck and Hess Questionnaire
subtitle: Neuroticism screening
description: This test helps you estimate your level of neuroticism in about five minutes.
algorithm: 1. You are given a list of statements.
2. For each statement, answer Yes if you agree (consider it true for you) or No if you disagree.
comment: If you have any concerns, consult a qualified specialist.
questions: Do you feel inwardly tense?
I am often so absorbed in something that I cannot fall asleep.
I feel easily hurt.
I find it hard to talk to strangers.
Do you often feel indifferent and tired for no particular reason?
I often feel that people are critically examining me.
Are you often troubled by useless thoughts that will not leave your head even when you try?
I am quite nervous.
I feel that no one understands me.
I am quite irritable.
If people were not against me, my affairs would go more successfully.
I take unpleasant things too close to heart for too long.
Even the thought of possible failure worries me.
I have had very strange and unusual experiences.
Do you sometimes feel joyful, sometimes sad for no apparent reason?
Throughout the day I daydream and fantasize more than I should.
Is your mood easily changed?
I often struggle with myself not to show my shyness.
I would like to be as happy as other people seem to be.
I sometimes tremble or have chills.
Does your mood often change with or without a serious reason?
Do you sometimes feel afraid even when there is no real danger?
Criticism or reprimand hurts me deeply.
Sometimes I am so restless that I cannot sit still.
Do you sometimes worry too much about minor things?
I often feel dissatisfied.
I find it hard to concentrate on a task or work.
I do many things I later regret.
For the most part I am happy.
I am not confident enough in myself.
I suffer from a sense of inferiority.
Sometimes I feel completely worthless.
I often feel simply awful.
I analyze myself a lot.
Sometimes everything hurts.
I have oppressive moods.
Something is wrong with my nerves.
I find it hard to keep a conversation going when meeting someone.
The hardest struggle for me is the struggle with myself.
Do you sometimes feel that difficulties are great and insurmountable?

title: Hare Test
subtitle: Psychopathy screening
description: This test helps you estimate your level of psychopathy in about five minutes.
algorithm: 1. You are given a list of statements.
2. For each statement, give one of the suggested answers.
comment: If you have any concerns, consult a qualified specialist.
questions: I am never tongue-tied.
In important relationships, I am above most people.
I am prone to boredom.
I lie to smooth things over.
I deceive people in some matters.
I rarely feel guilty.
I am an emotional person.
I rarely become emotionally attached to others.
Others often pay for me.
I am impatient.
I lead a disorderly sex life.
I was a difficult child.
I find it hard to stay committed to long-term goals.
I am impulsive.
I often do work carelessly.
I try to avoid responsibility.
My romantic relationships usually fall apart quickly.
I committed several crimes as a juvenile.
I violated probation.
I committed several types of crimes.
I am neither shy nor timid; I speak with authority and confidence.
I am exceptional.
I need risk to feel alive.
I am mostly an honest person.
I feel bad when I deceive people.
If someone deserves punishment, I do not feel too bad about it.
I think strong emotions are for the weak.
I think if people get offended, that is their problem.
I always look out for myself.
I never act rashly.
I think sex should not be taken lightly.
I often got into trouble at school.
I lack direction in my life.
I never give in to temptation.
I always keep my word.
My problems are mostly other people's fault.
I do not like commitments in relationships.
I was a bully at school.
I showed disrespect to the court.
I would not be proud of having committed crimes.

title: L.G. Pochebut Questionnaire
subtitle: Aggressiveness screening
description: This test helps you estimate your level of aggressiveness in about five minutes.
algorithm: 1. You are given a list of statements.
2. For each statement, give one of the suggested answers.
comment: If you have any concerns, consult a qualified specialist.
questions: During an argument I often raise my voice.
If someone annoys me, I may say everything I think about them.
If I need physical force to defend my rights, I will use it without hesitation.
When I meet someone unpleasant, I may pinch or push them unnoticed.
Caught up in an argument, I may bang my fist on the table to get attention or prove I am right.
I constantly feel that others do not respect my rights.
Remembering the past, I sometimes feel hurt for myself.
Though I do not show it, envy sometimes gnaws at me.
If I disapprove of acquaintances' behavior, I tell them directly.
In strong anger I use harsh words and swear.
If someone raises a hand against me, I try to strike first.
I sometimes get so furious that I throw things.
I often feel the need to rearrange furniture or completely change it.
In communication I often feel like a powder keg ready to explode.
I sometimes feel like playing a mean joke on someone.
When I am angry, I usually become gloomy.
In conversation I try to listen carefully without interrupting.
In my youth my fists often itched and I was always ready to use them.
If I know someone deliberately pushed me, it may come to a fight.
Creative clutter on my desk helps me work effectively.
I remember being so angry that I grabbed whatever was at hand and broke it.
Sometimes people irritate me just by being present.
I am often surprised by hidden motives behind someone's good deeds toward me.
If someone offends me, I lose the desire to talk to anyone.
I sometimes deliberately say nasty things about people I dislike.
When furious, I shout the nastiest insults.
In childhood I avoided fighting.
I know when and why it is okay to hit someone.
When angry, I may slam the door.
It seems to me that people around me do not like me.
I constantly share my feelings and experiences with others.
Very often my words and actions harm me.
When people yell at me, I yell back.
If someone hits me first, I hit them back.
It irritates me when things are not in their place.
If I cannot fix a broken item, in anger I destroy it completely.
Other people always seem more successful to me.
When I think of someone I dislike, I may get excited with the desire to harm them.
Sometimes it seems fate played a cruel joke on me.
If someone treats me badly, I become very upset about it.
